0.original: Big success calls for persons who continually set higher standards for themselves and others,

1.original: persons who are searching for ways to increase efficiency, to get more output at lower cost,

3.original: Top success is reserved for the I-can-do-it-better kind of person.

4.original: General Electric uses the slogan, Progress is our most important product.

5.original: Why not make progress your most important product?

7.original: When you ask yourself, how can I do better?

8.original: Your creative power is switched on and ways for doing things better suggest themselves.

9.original: Here is a daily exercise that will help you discover and develop the power of the I-can-do-better attitude.

10.original: Each day, before you begin work, devote 10 minutes to thinking, how can I do a better job today?

11.original: Ask, what can I do today to encourage my employees?

12.original: What special favor can I do for my customers?

13.original: How can I increase my personal efficiency?

14.original: This exercise is simple, but it works.

15.original: Try it, and you'll find unlimited creative ways to win greater success.

16.original: Just about every time my wife and I would get together with a certain couple,

17.original: the conversation would turn to working wives.

18.original: Mrs. S had worked several years before her marriage, and she had genuinely liked it.

19.original: But now, she'd say, I've got two youngsters in school, a home to manage, and meals to prepare.

21.original: Then one Sunday evening, Mr. and Mrs. S and their children were in an automobile accident.

22.original: Mrs. S and the youngsters escaped serious injury, but Mr. S received a back injury that left him permanently disabled.

23.original: Now Mrs. S had no choice but to go to work.

24.original: When we saw her several months after the accident, we were amazed to find how well she had adjusted to her new responsibilities.

25.original: You know, she said, six months ago I never dreamed I could possibly manage the house and work full time.

26.original: But after the accident, I just made up my mind that I had to find the time.

27.original: Believe me, my efficiency has gone up 100%.

28.original: I discovered a lot of things I was doing didn't need to be done at all.

29.original: Then I discovered that the children could and wanted to help.

30.original: I found dozens of ways to conserve time.

31.original: Fewer trips to the store, less TV, less telephoning, less of those time killers.

32.original: This experience teaches us a lesson.

33.original: Capacity is a state of mind.

34.original: How much we can do depends on how much we think we can do.

35.original: When you really believe you can do more, your mind thinks creatively and shows you the way.
